heading: Payment adjustment for health care-acquired conditions
label: 1396b–1 Payment adjustment for health care-acquired conditions
notes: References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in subsec. (c), is , . Title XIX of the Act is classified generally to this subchapter. CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act, and not as part of the Social Security Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services (in this subsection referred to as the “Secretary”) shall identify current State practices that prohibit payment for health care-acquired conditions and shall incorporate the practices identified, or elements of such practices, which the Secretary determines appropriate for application to the Medicaid program in regulations. Such regulations shall be effective as of , and shall prohibit payments to States under section 1903 of the Social Security Act [] for any amounts expended for providing medical assistance for health care-acquired conditions specified in the regulations. The regulations shall ensure that the prohibition on payment for health care-acquired conditions shall not result in a loss of access to care or services for Medicaid beneficiaries.July 1, 201142 U.S.C. 1396b

children.heading: Health care-acquired condition
children.label: b Health care-acquired condition
children.text: In this section. the term “health care-acquired condition” means a medical condition for which an individual was diagnosed that could be identified by a secondary diagnostic code described in section 1886(d)(4)(D)(iv) of the Social Security Act ().142 U.S.C. 1395ww(d)(4)(D)(iv)So in original. The period probably should be a comma.1

children.heading: Medicare provisions
children.label: c Medicare provisions
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary shall apply to State plans (or waivers) under title XIX of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] the regulations promulgated pursuant to section 1886(d)(4)(D) of such Act () relating to the prohibition of payments based on the presence of a secondary diagnosis code specified by the Secretary in such regulations, as appropriate for the Medicaid program. The Secretary may exclude certain conditions identified under title XVIII of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] for non-payment under title XIX of such Act when the Secretary finds the inclusion of such conditions to be inapplicable to beneficiaries under title XIX.42 U.S.C. 139642 U.S.C. 1395ww(d)(4)(D)42 U.S.C. 1395
